text: AI legal research platforms (Casetext CoCounsel, Westlaw Precision, Lexis+ AI, vLex Vincent AI) use LLMs and NLP for: semantic case law search (finding relevant precedents beyond keyword matching), citation analysis (Shepardizing -- tracking whether a case has been overturned), litigation analytics (predicting judge rulings, case duration from historical data), and AI-generated legal memos. CoCounsel (2023) was the first AI legal assistant to pass the Uniform Bar Exam.

AI litigation analytics: Lex Machina (LexisNexis) and Pre/Dicta analyze millions of court records to predict: probability of motion grant, expected case duration, judge behavior patterns (grant rates, trial tendencies), and likely damages range. Law firms using AI litigation analytics report 15-25% improvement in case strategy decisions and settlement timing accuracy.
